sender.what_they_sell: Executive response generation. Deep-researched, one-of-one physical pieces (A2 newspaper front pages and claymation scenes) printed on rigid foam board, packaged in premium boxes, and delivered to senior B2B buyers' desks. The piece earns attention, the follow-up converts it.
sender.proof_points: First Sentrada campaign (June 2026): 20 one-of-one pieces to VC partners, 8 responses in the first week; one founder has his piece on his office wall. Secondary record: responses from CEOs of Starbucks, L'Oreal and Danone, a meeting with the CEO of VINCI Construction, 10 years of enterprise outbound. First client doubled their order from 10 to 20 pieces before anything shipped.
problems: Enterprise pipeline is unpredictable because outbound to senior buyers has stopped working: email response rates are collapsing, cold calls don't reach C-suite, and the accounts that matter most are the hardest to engage
Sales teams are sending more volume with less result: AI has made personalised email easy to send and impossible to notice, so the highest-value accounts get buried in noise alongside everyone else
No-decision drag on enterprise deals: even when meetings happen, the lack of a memorable first impression means deals stall because the buyer has no emotional anchor to the relationship

email_postscript: Yes, I printed an email at A2 and had it delivered to your desk.
